ACRONYM TABLE 
Acronym Description 
GREP Global Reanalysis multi model Ensemble Product 
GLORYS2V4 GLObal Ocean ReanalYsiS 
CGLORS CMCC Global Ocean Physical Reanalysis System 
ORAS5 Ocean ReAnalysis System 
OSTIA Operational Sea Surface Temperature and Ice Analysis 
SST Sea Surface Temperature 
SSH Sea Surface Heigh 
SSS Sea Surface Salinity 
EKE Eddy Kinetic Energy 
NEMO Nucleus for European Modelling of the Ocean 
 
KEY DEFINITION 
 
Term Definition 
SST The skin measurement of the ocean’s surface temperature. 
SSS The skin measurement of the ocean’s surface salinity. 
SSH The skin measurement of the ocean’s surface height. 
MLD The depth over which surface fluxes have been recently  mixed and 
integrated and is a characteristic timescale of air-sea interactions. 
OHC Ocean Heat Content  is the surplus heat in Earth’s system from climate 
change stored in the ocean. 
OSC Ocean Salt Content is the quantity of salt in the ocean  
SIC Defined as the sea surface area covered by a significant amount of sea -
ice.  
SIT Sea Ice Thickness is the volume of sea ice in cubic kilometres. 
SIE Sea-ice extent is defined as the area of the ocean covered with more than 
15 % sea-ice concentration. 
EKE The energy associated with the turbulent part of the flow of a fluid. 
STD Mean square quadratic value difference compared to the mean.
